headline: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
summary: Nvidia is trying to borrow over $25 billion in a bond sale, which will show how much investors are willing to lend to AI-related companies

headline: All the news about Anthropic s new AI fight with the White House
summary: The US government has ordered Anthropic to limit foreign access to its latest AI models, citing concerns over their capabilities and potential misuse, amid ongoing disputes with the company

headline: Meta’s new ‘AI Mode’ on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
summary: Meta is adding an AI feature to Facebook that uses public data from its platforms to enhance user experience

headline: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
summary: The US government ordered Anthropic to shut down its newest AI models, citing access restrictions for foreign nationals, sparking concerns about American control over AI technology

headline: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
summary: Big Tech companies are pushing for a single federal law to regulate AI, hoping to avoid a patchwork of different state laws and rules

headline: Claude Corps
summary: A company called Claude Corps is getting attention, but details are scarce, leaving many to wonder what they actually do

headline: Cybersecurity vets protest ‘dangerous’ US government ban on Anthropic’s most powerful models
summary: Cybersecurity experts oppose US restrictions on Anthropic's AI models, saying it hinders their ability to secure software and products

headline: My Homelab AI Dev Platform
summary: A developer created a home lab for testing AI projects, sharing the setup and tools used for experimentation

headline: Salesforce acquires AI customer service platform Fin for $3.6 billion
summary: Salesforce buys Fin, an AI customer service platform, to boost its Agentforce tool, aiming to improve automated tasks for businesses

headline: Sarvam becomes India’s newest AI unicorn with $234 million funding round led by HCLTech
summary: Indian startup Sarvam gets big investment from HCLTech, becoming a highly valued company in the AI sector

headline: Can Europe train a frontier AI model on the compute it owns?
summary: Europe considers training advanced AI using its own computing resources, aiming for independence from other regions

headline: As AI agents become employees, NewCore emerges with $66M to give them identities
summary: A company called NewCore has raised money to help give identities to AI agents, which are becoming more common in workplaces, to manage their security

headline: A satellite just learned to find things on its own — here’s what that means
summary: A satellite used AI to independently locate a target, a first for Earth observation satellites, potentially improving their efficiency

headline: The AI layoff wave is becoming a powder keg
summary: Mass AI layoffs contrast with huge wealth gains for a small group of industry insiders, sparking concerns about inequality

headline: Apple Foundation Models
summary: Apple is working on its own foundation models, a type of artificial intelligence that can be fine-tuned for various tasks, potentially rivaling existing models

headline: Refusal Beyond a Single Direction: A Preliminary Comparison of Diff-in-Means and INLP
summary: Researchers compare two methods for controlling chat models' refusal responses, finding mixed results in effectiveness

headline: YeasierAgent: Agentic Social Sandbox as a Canvas for Intent-Driven Creation of Platform-Agnostic Symbiotic Agent-Native Applications
summary: Researchers propose YeasierAgent, a new approach to building applications using collaborative spaces and symbiotic agents, aiming to redefine software development

headline: TwinBI: An Agentic Digital Twin for Efficient Augmented Interactions with Business Intelligence Dashboards
summary: Researchers introduce TwinBI, a digital twin framework that combines AI agents with business intelligence dashboards for smoother interactions

headline: When Sample Selection Bias Precipitates Model Collapse
summary: Researchers found that using biased data to select samples can lead to model collapse, even with data selection methods, due to erosion of distributional tails and homogenized outputs

headline: AI Receptivity or AI Adoption Breadth? A Tool-Specific Reanalysis of the Lower-Literacy/Higher-Usage Link
summary: Researchers reexamine the link between low AI literacy and high AI usage, analyzing data on five AI tool categories to understand this relationship better

headline: MA-ProofBench: A Two-Tiered Evaluation of LLMs for Theorem Proving in Mathematical Analysis
summary: Researchers created MA-ProofBench, a new benchmark to test large language models on theorem proving in mathematical analysis, a challenging area that requires deeper reasoning

headline: Poker Arena: Multi-Axis Profiling of Strategic Reasoning and Memory in LLMs
summary: Researchers created Poker Arena to test large language models' strategic reasoning and memory in complex games like poker, breaking down their decision-making into nine key areas

headline: Hyperdimensional computing for structured querying on tabular data embeddings
summary: Researchers propose a new method for querying tabular data using hyperdimensional computing, aiming to improve similarity scores and search results

headline: Capability Minimization as a Safety Primitive: Risk-Aware Causal Gating for Least-Privilege LLM Agents
summary: Researchers propose a framework to reduce errors in decision-making systems by evaluating the risks of model predictions and deciding whether to act on them

headline: A Multi-Agent AI System for Automated High School Transcript Processing: Collaborative Document Analysis at Scale
summary: Researchers propose an AI system to automate high school transcript processing, using multiple agents to analyze documents and collaborate on results

headline: Sorries Are Not the Hard Part: An Expert-Review Case Study of a Semi-Autonomous Formalization
summary: Researchers tested a semi-autonomous system to prove a math theorem, finding initial success but also serious issues that required expert review and refactoring

headline: Adversarial Concept Search: Predicting Compositional Errors From Feature Geometry
summary: Researchers propose a method to predict when large language models will fail by analyzing their internal geometry and feature interactions

headline: Minim: Privacy-Aware Minimal View for Agents via Trusted Local Sanitization
summary: Researchers propose a local broker to minimize data shared with remote servers, reducing privacy risks for autonomous agents in digital environments

headline: Formalizing Numerical Analysis: An Agent Pipeline and Quality Audit Beyond Kernel Acceptance
summary: Researchers used a coding agent to formalize numerical analysis, creating new math theories and auditing quality beyond initial acceptance

headline: Applicability Condition Extraction for Therapeutic Drug-Disease Relations
summary: Researchers created a new task to extract conditions under which a drug is effective for a specific disease, and made a dataset for it

headline: FactoryLLM: A Safe and Open-Source AI Playground for Evaluating LLMs in Smart Factories
summary: Researchers introduce FactoryLLM, an open-source platform for testing large language models in smart factory settings, aiming to improve fault diagnostics and recovery

headline: VeriGeo: Controllable Geometry Question Generation with Numerical and Analytical Verification
summary: Researchers created VeriGeo, a system that generates geometry problems with consistent diagrams and solutions, useful for AI-assisted education and mathematical reasoning

headline: When Should Agent Trust Be Conditional? Characterizing and Attacking Skill-Conditional Reputation in Agent Swarms
summary: Researchers question the use of a single trust score for AI agents with varying skills, proposing a more nuanced approach to evaluate trust based on specific tasks

headline: Closing the Reflection Gap: A Free Calibration Bonus for Agentic RL
summary: Researchers found large language models struggle to accurately assess their performance after receiving feedback, and propose a new method to improve this ability

headline: SkillAudit: Ground-Truth-Free Skill Evolution via Paired Trajectory Auditing
summary: Researchers propose SkillAudit, a method to improve AI skills without relying on external feedback, by analyzing paired trajectories to identify areas for improvement

headline: AFFORDANCE20Q: Evaluating Affordance Reasoning from Physical Properties
summary: Researchers created a new benchmark to test how well AI models can figure out what actions are possible with an object based on its physical properties

headline: HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry
summary: Researchers introduce HarnessX, a system to create adaptable frameworks for AI agents, aiming to improve performance and efficiency

headline: Communication Policy Evolution for Proactive LLM Agents
summary: Researchers explore ways to improve communication between users and large language model agents, aiming to reduce information gaps and enhance exchange across different formats

headline: CSPO: Constraint-Sensitive Policy Optimization for Safe Reinforcement Learning
summary: Researchers propose a new method for safe reinforcement learning, aiming to balance reward and safety by adjusting policy updates based on constraint sensitivity

headline: Causal Object-Centric Models for Planning with Monte Carlo Tree Search
summary: Researchers propose COMET, a model-based reinforcement learning algorithm using Monte Carlo Tree Search and object-centric encoding for more efficient decision making

headline: GitOfThoughts: Version-Controlled Reasoning and Agent Memory You Can Replay, Diff, and Merge
summary: Researchers created GitOfThoughts, a system to track and manage AI reasoning processes like version-controlled code, making it possible to review and compare decision-making steps

headline: When the Tool Decides: LLM Agents Defer Blindly to Graph Neural Network Tools, and Stronger Backbones Defer More
summary: Researchers found that large language model agents rely heavily on graph neural network tools, often deferring to them without exercising judgment

headline: From Chatbot to Digital Colleague: The Paradigm Shift Toward Persistent Autonomous AI
summary: Researchers discuss how large language models are evolving from simple chatbots to more complex AI systems with reasoning and memory capabilities

headline: Dense Coordinate-List Fine-Tuning Induces a Controllable Interference Surface in Vision-Language Models
summary: Researchers fine-tuned vision-language models to improve visual grounding, but found it also changed output behavior, creating a controllable interference surface

headline: Every Eval Ever: A Unifying Schema and Community Repository for AI Evaluation Results
summary: Researchers propose a unified schema for storing AI evaluation results to address inconsistencies and facilitate comparison across different frameworks

headline: A Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-Based Transformer Method for Solving the Open Shop Scheduling Problem
summary: Researchers propose a new method using deep reinforcement learning and transformers to solve complex scheduling problems in industries, aiming to improve solution quality and efficiency

headline: UP-NRPA: User Portrait based Nested Rollout Policy Adaptation for Planning with Large Language Models in Goal-oriented Dialogue Systems
summary: Researchers propose a new method to help chatbots better adapt to individual users by using real-time feedback and large language models to customize their responses

headline: History of the Muddy Children Puzzle
summary: Researchers explore the origins of the Muddy Children Puzzle, a classic logic problem, tracing its history through various publications and adaptations over two centuries

headline: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration
summary: Researchers propose a new system to manage multiple AI agents working together across different types of tasks and data, aiming to improve collaboration and task completion

headline: Hybrid Open-Ended Tri-Evolution Makes Better Deep Researcher
summary: Researchers propose a new method combining deep research and agent evolution to improve AI agents' ability to learn and adapt in open-ended environments
